title: Surfaces, Draw Order, Alpha, Transforms, Assets, and Presentation · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for surfaces, draw order, alpha, transforms, assets, and presentation and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, pygame-ce, SDL, display, input, audio, filesystem, packaging, GPU, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for owned layered rendering and asset pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, cau…
objectives: Distinguish display and off-screen surfaces, pixel formats, per-surface and per-pixel alpha, color keys, locks, and conversion timing.
Define clear, background, world, effects, HUD, focus, diagnostics, and presentation order once per frame.
Rotate and scale from an immutable source, recenter output bounds, cache justified variants, and avoid cumulative degradation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Render Alternatives
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Asteroids team handles case 4529 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to compose draw order, surfaces, alpha, asset conversion, transforms, and one explicit frame presentation; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Make ship, hazard, projectile, focus, damage, and status distinguishable without color alone and under reduced effects.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Render Alternatives.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for make ship, hazard, projectile, focus, damage, and status distinguishable without color alone and under reduced effects. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Render Alternatives, keep Render Alternatives connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

stimulus.title: Render Alternatives failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A neon palette is readable and accessible by default.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Make ship, hazard, projectile, focus, damage, and status distinguishable without color alone and under reduced effects.

options.text: Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.

For diagnose the hidden failure, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Render Alternatives
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Render Alternatives: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Asteroids team handles case 3624 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to compose draw order, surfaces, alpha, asset conversion, transforms, and one explicit frame presentation; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Make ship, hazard, projectile, focus, damage, and status distinguishable without color alone and under reduced effects.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Make ship, hazard, projectile, focus, damage, and status distinguishable without color alone and under reduced effects.
